zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Rohese de Clare於1252年10月17日出生在英國肯特托布利其時，她的父親Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester30歲，母親Maud de Lacy29歲。她於1270年7月15日之後與Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾瑟斯克結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1316年在英國英格蘭越可爾霍文加姆享年64歲，埋葬在英國越可爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Rohese de Clare于1252年10月17日出生在英国肯特托布利其时，她的父亲Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester30岁，母亲Maud de Lacy29岁。她于1270年7月15日之后与Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray在英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾瑟斯克结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1316年在英国英格兰越可爾霍文加姆享年64岁，埋葬在英国越可爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Rohese
meanings: An old British name. It is found Latinised Rhesus, and is probably derived from the Greek word for Mars, the god of war.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Rohese de Clare was born on 17 October 1252, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, her father, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, was 30 and her mother, Maud de Lacy, was 29. She married Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray after 15 July 1270, in Thirsk, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died in 1316, in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Rohese de Clare est née le 17 octobre 1252, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Maud de Lacy, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray après 15 juillet 1270, à Thirsk, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée en 1316, à Hovingham, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Everwyck, Angleterre.

Sie hat nach 15. Juli 1270 in Thirsk, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1316 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Hovingham, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Yorkshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Rohese de Clareが1252年10月17日にイギリスケントトンブリッジで生まれた時，父Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucesterは30歳，母Maud de Lacyは29歳であった。1270年7月15日以後にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアスイルスクでRoger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbrayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1316年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアホーヴィンガムで64歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Rohese de Clare(이)가 1252년 10월 17일 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Lacy(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1270년 7월 15일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 스이르스크에서 Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1316년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 호빙함에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Rohese de Clare nasceu em 17 de outubro de 1252, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Lacy, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray após 15 de julho de 1270, em Thirsk, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Rohese de Clare родилась 17 октября 1252 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Lacy, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray после 15 июля 1270 г. в местности тирск, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла в 1316 г. в местности ховингхам, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности еркшир, Англия.

Se casó con Roger de Mowbray 1st Baron Mowbray después de 15 de julio de 1270, en Thirsk, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1316, en Hovingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
